subject: Cloud Computing
title: What Is Virtualization?
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: What Is Virtualization?.

hook.text: One physical server running one app wastes 85% of its capacity. That's like driving a bus alone.

buildup.text: Virtualization lets one physical server pretend to be many smaller virtual servers.

discovery.text: Each virtual machine thinks it has its own CPU, memory, and storage. It's all an illusion.

twist.text: The idea existed since the 1960s. IBM mainframes could run virtual machines decades ago.

climax.text: Modern cloud computing is built on virtualization. Without it, the cloud couldn't exist.

punchline.text: One machine pretending to be many. That's the cloud's trick.

quiz.question: What is the core idea behind virtualization?
options: One physical machine runs multiple virtual machines simultaneously
Replacing all physical hardware with software programs
Connecting many small computers into one supercomputer
